headline: Medical Equipment Maintenance Backlog Idles 2,200 Machines Nationwide
context: Public hospitals have 2,200 medical equipment units out of service due to maintenance and spare parts procurement delays.

cards.big: 2,200 medical equipment units non-functional in public hospitals as of Q1 2026
cards.sub: MRI machines CT scanners and ventilators among idle equipment awaiting repair

cards.big: Average repair turnaround for major medical equipment is 4.8 months in public hospitals
cards.sub: Spare parts procurement through government channels creates bureaucratic delay

cards.big: 28% of public hospital MRI machines were non-operational at any given time in 2025
cards.sub: Patients referred to private facilities at government expense costing RM85 million annually

cards.big: Singapore's centralized medical equipment management achieves 96% uptime across all hospitals
cards.sub: Preventive maintenance contracts with guaranteed response times minimize downtime

cards.big: Expensive medical equipment sitting broken while patients wait is waste disguised as a procurement problem
Should medical equipment maintenance be outsourced to guaranteed-uptime service contracts
